summary: Created 6-phase implementation plan for modular logic architecture spanning 18 hours. Refactors existing Propositional and Modal formula types to align with BimodalLogic conventions, defines new Temporal and Bimodal formula types, establishes connective typeclass hierarchy, embedding functions with Coe instances, and proof system typeclass hierarchy following the FormalizedFormalLogic/Foundation pattern.

artifacts.summary: 6-phase implementation plan for modular logic architecture with connective typeclasses, formula refactoring, new formula types, embeddings, and proof system hierarchy

memory_candidates.content: When designing a modular logic architecture in Lean 4 with multiple logic levels (propositional, modal, temporal, bimodal), the established pattern from FormalizedFormalLogic/Foundation is: (1) each logic defines its own standalone Formula inductive with duplicated shared constructors since Lean 4 cannot extend inductives, (2) a shared typeclass hierarchy provides notation and polymorphic axiom definitions across formula types, (3) composition is achieved via explicit embedding functions with Coe instances rather than type-level extension, (4) proof system composition uses polymorphic axiom abbrevs + HasAxiom typeclasses on opaque tag types + bundled proof system classes via extends, (5) metalogic results (soundness, completeness) remain monolithic per logic because interaction axioms prevent cross-logic decomposition. This three-layer architecture (atomic typeclasses -> bundled connective classes -> concrete formula types) scales to 8+ logics in Foundation.
